author: @sofa-lofa4241

Next time I leave something out with a note on it, I'll make sure it says 'Free to a mad professor with a hacksaw'

author: @stevetobias4890
text: If you had that solid cross welded section on both sides it would help to have a vertical drum turbine on both sides to maintain a balanced rotation in high winds and a larger surface area in lower winds. Maybe even some sort of automatic rotor plane angle assembly to try to keep the speed within a certain limit to have the best output ratio for the generator assembly.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it was on both sides - I chopped one off for this model - but everyone makes their own choices mate - if you get one then do a two sided model for sure

author: @benthere8051
text:
LMAO - "Free to a good home" and basically what we're going to do is take it to pieces and saw it up.
I'm sure that's not what they meant.
But you can bet now it's going to serve a better purpose than someone that's going to banish it to the basement after two months of good intentions.
I enjoyed the video, keep up the good work.

replies.author: @UKBorder-Farce
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Conscience-pricking machines. Best turned into a device to salve your ecological conscience. Thank you Robert, yet another absolutely marvellous video project. I never tire of them, and your enthusiastic air of wonderment.Your videos will be inspiring people long after we've all turned to compost, ha ha! Best wishes, Mike.
